title.zh: 任何一幅面孔影像都可能成为追猎的钟声
title.en: The Face That Turns Any Image into a Summons

summary.zh: 一旦有人直接或间接看见其面孔，实体便陷入极端悲恸，随后以无法阻挡的速度追至观看者所在之处。
summary.en: A humanoid that enters unstoppable pursuit whenever anyone sees its face, even as a tiny detail in a distant photograph.

description.zh:
SCP-096是四肢异常修长、皮肤缺乏色素的人形实体，平时蜷缩、踱步或遮掩面部。危险并不取决于观看者是否理解所见：肉眼、录像或照片中哪怕只有数个像素，只要呈现了它的脸，观看者便成为目标。实体先以哭泣和尖叫进入强烈应激，随后直线追踪触发者，越过距离、地形与常规阻拦。事件结束后，它重新恢复消沉状态。

这套机制让图像本身成为延迟引信。一张风景照可以安静保存多年，直到有人放大背景；数据库里的缩略图也可能把收容失败复制到任何地点。SCP-096于是把现代视觉文化最基本的承诺反转：照片原本让远方安全地抵达眼前，这里却让远方实体沿着观看关系反向抵达观者。著名事故记录还揭露基金会内部有人故意安排突破以推动销毁提案，使档案不只讲异常怪物，也讲制度在“更安全的结果”名义下制造灾难的能力。
description.en:
SCP-096 is an elongated, unpigmented humanoid that ordinarily paces, crouches, or covers its face. Danger does not depend on whether an observer understands the image. Direct sight, video, or even a few pixels in the background of a photograph can designate a viewer as the target. The entity first enters a state of crying and screaming, then pursues that person in a direct line, overcoming distance, terrain, and conventional obstruction. After the event it returns to a withdrawn state.

This mechanism converts every image into a delayed fuse. A landscape photograph may remain harmless for years until someone enlarges the background; a database thumbnail can reproduce a containment failure anywhere. SCP-096 reverses the central promise of modern visual culture. Photography usually allows the distant world to arrive safely before the eye; here the distant entity follows the act of viewing back to the viewer. A major incident report also exposes personnel who deliberately engineered a breach to justify destruction, widening the horror from an anomalous creature to an institution capable of manufacturing catastrophe in pursuit of a supposedly safer result.

value.zh: 任何直接或间接的面部视觉信息
value.en: Any direct or mediated visual information of the face

sources.zh: SCP-096 原始档案，作者：Dr Dan
